title: libocedrus papuana subsp. arfakensis
intro: Libocedrus papuana subsp. arfakensis is a plant species found in the mountainous regions of Papua New Guinea, belonging to the cypress family (Cupressaceae) and known for its unique resinous properties. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Libocedrus papuana subsp. arfakensis include its ability to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing the leaves and resin in hot water or oil to create a topical treatment for various ailments.
